pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to target JDK 21.
Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version compatible with JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older compiler plugin versions may fail to compile or recognize JDK 21 language features and class files.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace separate source and target configuration with the unified release configuration.
pom.xml.why_exists: Using source/target with JDK 21 can lead to mismatched API usage or compilation errors if not aligned; release is the standard for modern JDKs.

registry-core-client/src/main/java/gr/uo.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with the new List.getFirst() method introduced in Java 21.
registry-core-client/src/main/java/gr/uo.why_exists: List.get(0) is deprecated for removal in Java 21; while it still compiles, the migration recipe enforces the use of the new API to avoid future breakage and deprecation warnings.

registry-core-elasticsearch/src/main/jav.general_idea: Replace String.format with String.formatted for logging message construction.
registry-core-elasticsearch/src/main/jav.before: logger.debug(String.format("@Retryable 'searchId(resourceType=%s, ids={%s})'", resourceType, String.join(",", Arrays.stream(fields).map(keyValue -> keyValue.getField() + "=" + keyValue.getValue()).collect(Collectors.toSet()))));

registry-core-elasticsearch/src/main/jav.general_idea: Replace String.format with String.formatted for exception message construction.
registry-core-elasticsearch/src/main/jav.before: throw new IllegalArgumentException(String.format("Quantity should be up to %s.", maxQuantity));
registry-core-elasticsearch/src/main/jav.after: throw new IllegalArgumentException("Quantity should be up to %s.".formatted(maxQuantity));

registry-core-rest/src/main/java/gr/uoa/.general_idea: Replace String.format with the String.formatted method introduced in Java 15.

registry-core/src/main/java/gr/uoa/di/ma.general_idea: Replace deprecated Paths.get() with Path.of() for Java 21 compatibility.
registry-core/src/main/java/gr/uoa/di/ma.why_exists: Paths.get() is deprecated for removal in Java 21; using it may cause compile-time deprecation warnings or errors depending on compiler settings, and Path.of() is the modern replacement.
